skill: Evaluating statistical claims: Observational studies and experiments
stem: For a maple grove in Vermont, a scientist randomly selected 60 maple trees that were 22 years old and randomly assigned them to two groups. Each group received a different fertilizer treatment for 3 consecutive years to observe whether the fertilizer affects sap yield. Based on the design of the study, what is the largest group to which these results can be applied?
rationale: The selection was random from 22-year-old maple trees in one specific grove. Results may be generalized only to the population from which the random sample was drawn: 22-year-old maple trees in this grove. Choice A is broader than the sampling frame. Choice C is too narrow given random sampling. Choice D refers to an age not sampled.
